entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: An important legume crop (Cicer arietinum L.) grown in the tropics and subtropics.

entities.name: PATHOGENS
entities.description: Biological agents that cause diseases, with more than 50 reported to affect chickpea.

entities.name: TROPICS AND SUBTROPICS
entities.description: Geographical regions where chickpea is an important crop.

entities.name: WILT
entities.description: A disease of economic importance affecting chickpea.

entities.name: DRY ROOT ROT
entities.description: A disease of economic importance affecting chickpea.

entities.name: ASCOCHYTA BLIGHT
entities.description: A disease of economic importance affecting chickpea.

entities.name: DISEASE RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of plant breeding material to resist pathogens.

entities.name: RESISTANCE-SCREENING TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Standardized procedures developed at ICRISAT to screen germplasm and breeding material for disease resistance.

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: The institution where resistance-screening techniques were developed and standardized.

entities.name: NINE-POINT RATING SCALE
entities.description: A scale used to facilitate the identification of resistant breeding material.
